# Foundry

> Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET

Author: Nick Cosentino (https://www.devleader.ca).

- [Home](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/) -- Build reliable .NET AI agents with generated discovery, workflows, observability, evaluation, and deterministic testing.
- [Getting Started](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/getting-started/) -- Build Foundry, choose the packages for your scenario, register the runtime, and create your first generated agent.
- [Configuration](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/configuration/) -- Understand Foundry's configuration surfaces for agents, workflows, diagnostics, evaluation, providers, and optional Needlr integration.
- [Building from Source](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/building/) -- Build, test, pack, and validate the Foundry repository from source.

## Guides

- [Overview](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/guides/) -- Choose a Foundry guide for orchestration, observability, evaluation, testing, or provider integration.

## Agent Framework

- [Integrations and Workflows](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/ai-integrations/) -- Build AI agent systems with Foundry in .NET using generated discovery, workflow orchestration, and optional Needlr integration.
- [Agent Functions](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/agent-functions/) -- Parameter optionality, default-value resolution, and DTO null-handling semantics for [AgentFunction]-decorated tools in Foundry's source-generated AIFunction wrappers.
- [Iterative Agent Loop](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/iterative-agent-loop/) -- Run multi-step agentic workloads with O(n) token cost using Foundry's iterative agent loop -- workspace-driven prompt construction eliminates conversation history accumulation.
- [Workspace](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/workspace/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
- [Progress Reporting](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/progress-reporting/) -- Track agent and workflow execution in real-time with Foundry's progress reporting system -- optional Needlr discovery, per-orchestration isolation, and ambient async context.
- [Pipeline Metrics](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/pipeline-metrics/) -- Pipeline-shape OpenTelemetry metrics and distributed-tracing spans emitted automatically by SequentialPipelineRunner — per-pipeline outcome, per-stage duration / tokens / tool-failures, parent + child activity spans.
- [GenAI Token Metrics](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/gen-ai-token-metrics/) -- Emit cache_read and reasoning measurements on the OpenTelemetry gen_ai.client.token.usage histogram so dashboards can answer "what's my prompt-cache hit ratio?" and "how many tokens went to reasoning vs final completion?" per LLM call.
- [Stage Termination](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/stage-termination/) -- Typed-case StageTermination hierarchy describes why a pipeline stage stopped — pattern-match in C#, low-cardinality OTel tags via ToTagValue, polymorphic JSON wire format.
- [Tool Result Middleware](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/tool-result-middleware/) -- How Foundry's ToolResultFunctionMiddleware translates tool exceptions and IToolResult returns into LLM-safe responses, and the trade-offs of enabling it.
- [Tiered Provider Selection](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/tiered-provider-selector/) -- Configure how TieredProviderSelector reacts to provider exceptions — match exception kinds, skip failing providers for a duration, run a callback on hit. Replaces the hardcoded ProviderUnavailableException catch with declarative, per-instance policies.

## Evaluation

- [Evaluation](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/evaluation/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
- [Experiment Runner](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/experiment-runner/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
- [Langfuse](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/langfuse/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET

## Providers

- [GitHub Copilot](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/copilot/) -- Use GitHub Copilot as an IChatClient provider in Foundry with web search tool support.

## Testing

- [Testing Tools](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/testing-tools/) -- Paved-path testing for [AgentFunction]-decorated tools. Use ToolInvocationRunner to exercise the source-generated AIFunction wrapper with seeded workspace state, zero hand-rolled DI, and the same code path FunctionInvokingChatClient takes in production.

## Analyzers

- [Overview](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/analyzers/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
- [FDRYMAF001](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/analyzers/FDRYMAF001/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET

- [Overview](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/api/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
- [Development](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/api/dev/) -- Browse API documentation generated from the latest Foundry main branch.
- [Stable](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/api/stable/) -- Browse API documentation for the latest published Foundry release.

## Architecture Decisions

- [ADR-0001 DAG Graph Workflow Support](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/adr/adr-0001-dag-graph-workflow-support/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
- [ADR-0002 Build ScriptedChatClient Locally](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/adr/adr-0002-build-scriptedchatclient-locally/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
- [ADR-0003 Provider-Neutral Experiment Runner](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/adr/adr-0003-provider-neutral-experiment-runner/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
- [ADR-0004 Extract AI Platform from Needlr](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/adr/adr-0004-extract-ai-platform-from-needlr/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
- [ADR-0005 First-Class MAF Harness Integration](https://www.devleader.ca/projects/foundry/adr/adr-0005-first-class-maf-harness-integration/) -- Build reliable, observable, and testable AI agent systems in .NET
